name: Kalamarakia Tursi (Pickled Squid)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and clean the squid, separating the outer sacs from the heads and tentacles. Remove and discard the translucent cartilage and small sand bag and ink.
2. Rub salt on the outer sacs and rinse them inside and out with cold water. Rinse the heads and tentacles thoroughly.
3. Cut the sacs into 1/2-inch wide rounds.
4. Heat the olive oil in a frying pan and cook the squid rounds, heads, and tentacles until they are bright pink and tender (approximately 30 minutes).
5. Add salt, pepper, parsley, and rosemary during the last 15 minutes of cooking.
6. Transfer the cooked squid and juices to a clean quart-sized jar.
7. Pour white vinegar almost to the top of the jar.
8. Add pickling spices and herbs.
9. Seal the jar tightly and shake well.
10. Marinate at least one day before serving.
11. Keep refrigerated. Serve cold within 10 days.
